title: viola × visseriana
intro: Viola × visseriana, also known as the hybrid pansy or Viola visseriana, is a hybrid plant derived from the intersection of two viola species. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Viola × visseriana can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, which are often used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
